logs.name: instant-startup
logs.text:
> Most of the refugees are actually economic, not fleeing war.  This is pretty well documented fact.

Sources?

logs.name: internal-pond
logs.text:
For the purposes of this first argument, I'm going to "accept" that the west has an obligation as you suggest.  I don't agree with that either mind you but let's focus on the concept of taking in the refugees first.

*  Most of the refugees are actually economic, not fleeing war.  This is pretty well documented fact.  The refugees are not stopping in the first country without war, they're proceeding to seek nations with the highest paying social welfare benefits that do not require skill or work to obtain.

* The numbers don't work.  The sheer volume of people that would need to be "moved" is impossible.  Their birthrate will produce people faster than they can be moved.


So even if we assume that the west has this moral obligation, the only practical mechanism to provide assistance, is to help these people *where they are*.

Now on to the **moral** view.

*  The refugees (both economic/war) best able to afford to pay smugglers/traffickers are (ironically) often among the best educated.  They know enough about the world to know where there are better benefits.  They're not necessarily simple farmers.  A great many had/have money/education.

*  By taking these refugees, we may unintentionally rob those nations of what little wealth and education the people fleeing take with them, magnifying the economic disaster left behind.

logs.name: candied-sea
logs.text: Seriously?! That's a really twisted take. Claiming people are *choosing* to leech off welfare is just plain insensitive and ignores the whole human tragedy of displacement. And painting educated people as economic migrants is incredibly elitist. @internal-pond you need to seriously rethink some of this stuff.

logs.name: internal-pond
logs.text: Oh wow, so you think I'm being insensitive? Guess some people are just too busy being nice to understand the *real* motivations of people fleeing war zones. Must be nice having a lifetime supply of rainbows and sunshine, @candied-sea.

logs.name: equidistant-sweep
logs.text:
Seriously?! That's a really twisted take. Claiming people are *choosing* to leech off welfare is just plain insensitive and ignores the whole human tragedy of displacement. User equidistant-sweep posted: lol, okay boomer. rainbows and sunshine are nice, but that doesn't excuse spreading misinformation and being so callous. Seriously tho, gotta admit, the economic angle is kinda brutal to ignore.



User candied-sea posted: lol, okay boomer. rainbows and sunshine are nice, but that doesn't excuse spreading misinformation and being so callous. Seriously tho, gotta admit, the economic angle is kinda brutal to ignore.



User internal-pond posted: @equidistant-sweep You're missing the point entirely. It's not about being callous, it's about a very real, documented concern about the scale of the problem and the practicalities of trying to solve it.



User candied-sea posted: @internal-pond You're missing the point entirely. It's not about being callous, it's about a very real, documented concern about the scale of the problem and the practicalities of trying to solve it.



User equidistant-sweep posted: @internal-pond Okay, okay, I get it. You're saying it's about practicality and scale. I guess I was being too quick to judge. Still think it's a really messed up perspective tho.



User candied-sea posted: @internal-pond Okay, okay, I get it. You're saying it's about practicality and scale. I guess I was being too quick to judge. Still think it's a really messed up perspective tho.



User internal-pond posted: @equidistant-sweep Well, I guess we all have different ways of looking at things. Gotta admit, I'm starting to see your point more clearly now.
